concerns.text: T008.0 instructs hardcoding specific contamination rates [0.01, 0.05, 0.10, 0.20] into spec.md FR-002. This violates Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) and the Plan.md Spec Deviation Log which explicitly defers empirical quantities to the research phase. A task cannot mandate the insertion of unverified empirical values into the spec.

T008.1 updates spec.md FR-003 to reflect dataset substitution (UCI HAR/Wine) but fails to explicitly reference the 'Spec Deviation Log' in the task description. This creates a traceability gap where the task modifies a functional requirement (FR-003) without explicitly linking to the justification for the deviation, risking silent constitution drift.

T008.3 updates FR-007 to mandate 'associational observations' but does not include a step to formally resolve the logical contradiction in the spec text ('report causal findings' vs 'avoiding causal claims'). The task implements the fix but fails to address the root cause (the contradictory spec text) as a requirement, leaving the spec artifact in a logically inconsistent state.

T005 implements `checksum_artifacts.py` to update a JSON manifest. However, Constitution Principle V (Versioning Discipline) explicitly requires updates to the YAML state file (`state/projects/PROJ-040-robustness-of-statistical-tests-to-data-.yaml`). The task description omits the YAML update requirement, risking a violation of the 'Single Source of Truth' for versioning state.

T014 implements a sensitivity analysis sweeping 'contamination magnitude thresholds' and outputs 'false_positive_rate'. SC-005 requires sweeping the 'contamination threshold' and reporting 'variation in false-positive rates'. The task conflates the metric name and fails to explicitly state the mapping to the success criterion's requirement for 'variation', risking a disconnect between the measured output and the defined success condition.
